mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.07% is near the zero lower bound, and the market anticipates eventual normalization as the economy recovers, causing long-term yields to rise over the next 20 days as expectation of future tightening builds.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with recovery expectations
failure_condition: The 20-day forward yield change is negative in more than 60% of instances when the fed funds rate is ≤0.10%, or rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate from its current elevated level of 6.7% signals labor market improvement, which raises inflation expectations and pushes long-term yields higher as the market prices in tighter monetary policy ahead.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by at least 0.2 percentage points in a single month
failure_condition: When UNRATE drops by ≥0.2pp in a month, the 20-day forward yield change is negative in over half the cases, or hit rate < 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Strengthening consumer demand, proxied by rising retail sales and housing starts, signals a robust economy that will lead to higher inflation and thus higher long-term yields as the market anticipates less accommodative policy.
failure_condition: The combined increase in RSAFS and HOUST does not lead to positive yield changes in at least 55% of months over a 3-year window, or the correlation between the joint indicator and yield changes is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in industrial production indicates stronger economic activity, which feeds into higher aggregate demand and inflation, causing the 10-year yield to increase as real rates and inflation expectations rise.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO increases by more than 0.5% month-over-month
failure_condition: After INDPRO rises >0.5%, the 20-day yield change is negative in >50% of occurrences, or the average yield change is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: With the federal funds rate at the effective lower bound, any economic weakening or disinflationary pressure will cause long-term yields to decline as investors expect prolonged accommodation and a risk-off environment.
evidence_refs: fact:fed funds at zero lower bound
regime_hint: zero lower bound with deflation risk
failure_condition: When fed funds is ≤0.10%, yields rise in more than 50% of 20-day windows, contradicting the predicted negative direction.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-unemployment regime (UNRATE > 6.0%), a positive surprise in industrial production (INDPRO) signals that the recovery is gaining traction, leading to upward revisions in growth expectations and higher yields.
failure_condition: The joint condition produces positive yield changes in less than 60% of occurrences over a 5-year period, or the average yield change is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: When both the unemployment rate declines and the consumer price index rises simultaneously, it signals a tightening labor market and rising inflation, which forces the bond market to price in higher term premiums, pushing yields upward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines and CPIAUCSL increases month-over-month
regime_hint: late expansion or recovery with inflation
failure_condition: When UNRATE falls and CPI rises, the 20-day yield change is negative in more than 40% of cases, or the average yield change is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts signals weakening demand in the housing sector, which is an early indicator of broader economic slowdown, leading to lower growth expectations and a decline in long-term yields as investors seek safety.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 850 (thousands of units) from its current level
failure_condition: After HOUST drops below 850, the 60-day forward yield change is positive in more than 50% of occurrences, or the negative yield change frequency is below 50%.
